Summary: AI designed new antivenoms for a kind of snakebite (three-finger toxins) that currently only has an expensive and slow treatment.
Commentary: Actually a pipeline of open models: uses a Baker Lab model, RFDiffusion, to design the protein backbone, then ProteinMPNN to design the sequence, and then screens the designs in silico using AlphaFold2 and Rosettafold. Only n=15 mice

Summary: AI method (CNN) to design complex high-frequency electronic circuits much faster and more efficiently than traditional methods
Commentary: "universal inverse design approach for arbitrary-shaped complex multi-port electromagnetic structures with designer radiative and scattering properties, co-designed with active circuits" "CNN based forward model for prediction of scattering parameters of arbitrary shaped passive EM structures, and an approach to inverse synthesis utilizing this forward model"

Summary: Formalisation of the Strong Prime Number Theorem
Commentary: AI was involved in formalising a very nontrivial result with 1000 lemmas and 25000 lines of Lean code. A very nontrivial result (1000 lemmas, 25 KLOC). Many hundreds of hours of human effort, so call this a Tao-Lichtman result with crucial help from the AI

Summary: Humans (n=4 chess grandmasters) learned new useful concepts extracted automatically from AlphaZero
Commentary: Humans learning usable superhuman concepts in chess

Summary: Google's Big Sleep AI found another nasty vulnerability in SQLite, its second in a year.
Commentary: Actually useful AI: Google's Big Sleep found another nasty vuln in SQLite, its second in a year. Other AI security companies are finding these routinely now.c

Summary: Driverless cars are 80% safer than human-driven cars.
Commentary: Driverless cars are 80% safer than human-driven cars.

Summary: A multi-agent system for automating scientific discovery
scientists warn of threat posed by AI-generated microscopy images
viruses that modify themselves via LLM API calls
Commentary: Evo and ESM-3 are genomic and protein LMs. You could do things with these

Summary: Claude has been outperforming nearly all of its human competitors in basic hacking competitions with minimal human assistance and little-to-no effort.
"an AI clinical copilot"
Commentary: Actually useful AI

Summary: First decipherment of a pre-Islam Eastern South Semitic script.
Commentary: (One of the two subtypes anyway)

Summary: Neanderthals in Germany were processing hundreds of big animals for bone marrow 125,000 years ago in a kind of industry.
Commentary: Grease rendering is forever. Neanderthals in Germany were processing hundreds of big animals for bone marrow 125,000 years ago, a kind of industry. Likely boiled bones in organic containers. Difficult to distinguish from later processing.

Summary: The Native Americans may have originated in Japan, arriving in the Americas 20,000 years ago
The oldest baby in history: an embryo frozen in 1994 was brought to term and resulted in Thaddeus Pierce, a healthy baby boy.
Commentary: His mother Lindsey is in some sense only 5 years older than him. The previous record was 25 years.

Summary: An Australian team found RuBisCO mutations that boosted tobacco growth by 50% when the enzyme was injected.
Commentary: The most common enzyme on Earth is RuBisCO, crucial to photosynthesis and therefore to most plant growth. It’s weirdly inefficient. An Australian team used a bacterium to screen Rubisco mutations, finding two big ones. Genetic engineering failed, but just injecting the enzyme A242V boosted tobacco growth by 50%.

Summary: A new class of treatment for malaria. GanLum cured 97% of patients and shouldn't suffer from existing drug resistances (it kills parasites with resistance to existing antibiotics). The big hope is that it also blocks transmission
Commentary: The previous best treatment was already 94% effective. "the first new class of malaria drug approved in more than 25 years.". See also the identification of a new family of natural antibodies.

Summary: Tooth regrowing procedure enters human trials
Commentary: Treatment for congenital anadontia (6 or more teeth missing / never growing). In mice, mutation / knockout of USAG-1 causes development of supernumerary teeth. Further experiments in mice showed that USAG-1 is a valid therapeutic target. Researchers at Kitano Hospital in Japan developmed neutralizing antibodies against USAG-1 (TRG035). First-in-human studies are currently underway in healthy adult volunteers.

Summary: Genetic design of new flowers is now a viable hobby
5,562 proteins were found to have tissue-specific enrichment across 32 human tissues.
Commentary: What is the brain doing

Summary: A bacteria identified as having a possible causal role in major depressive disorder
A smart pattern detector for enzyme cleavage sites in proteins.
A battery-free nanofluidic intracellular delivery patch for internal organs
A textbook assumption about the brain’s most abundant receptors needs to be rewritten
Gene therapy for heritably high cholesterol.
Commentary: Gene therapy for heritably high cholesterol

Summary: Mitochondrial Donation and Preimplantation Genetic Testing for mtDNA Disease
The oldest protein ever sequenced was previously 4 million years old, but now a 20 million year old protein has been sequenced.
Commentary: Previously the oldest protein ever sequenced was 4 million years old. Now it's 20mya.

Summary: The clap is out.
Baby Is Healed With World’s First Personalized Gene-Editing Treatment
A natural experiment provides evidence that the shingles vaccine protects against dementia with a 3.5% absolute risk reduction.
Commentary: Natural experiment gives evidence that the shingles vaccine protects against dementia, 3.5% absolute risk reduction. 7 year follow-up on 79 year olds vs 80 year olds.

Summary: Automated acoustic monitoring of bird species with full natural language understanding can answer questions like "how many babies are there in earshot?"
Commentary: Automated acoustic monitoring of bird species with full natural language understanding (“how many babies are there in earshot?”)

Summary: There's a pacemaker smaller than a grain of rice.
Suzetrigine is the first oral non-opioid painkiller approved for use in the US.
Commentary: Suzetrigine is the first oral non-opioid painkiller approved for use in the US. It’s a sodium-channel inhibitor. It's strong, as strong as hydrocodone or low-dose morphine. It is claimed to be nonaddictive, which is the somewhat unlikely part. It does hit the brain much less, which might work.

Summary: We now know when aerobic bacteria arose.
Commentary: We now know when aerobic bacteria arose. Aerobic metabolism emerged 3.2 Ga before the Great Oxidation Event, predating photosynthesis. Clock calibration uncertainties and model assumptions (especially horizontal gene transfer rates) limit confidence. 65%

Summary: Preprint shows epigenetic rejuvenation in primary cells by manipulating the expression of just one gene, reversing "ten years" of aging in one month.
Commentary: Preprint shows epigenetic rejuvenation in primary cells by manipulating the expression of just one gene. “Ten years” of reversed ageing in one month of overexpression. This is as measured by “epigenetic clocks”, a proxy for actual aging.

Summary: First partial restoration of sight after macular degeneration using eye implants and special glasses in a clinical trial.
Commentary: First partial restoration of sight after macular degeneration. Eye implants and some special glasses, clinical trial with nonrandom n=32, 1 year followup.

Summary: Wild chimps ingest alcohol equivalent to a pint of lager a day (via eating fermented fruit).
First real test of mitochondrial replacement therapy IVF resulted in 8 healthy babies born to mothers with dangerous mitochondrial genes.
Commentary: First real test of mitochondrial replacement therapy IVF. n=8 healthy babies born to mothers with dangerous mitrochondrial homoplasmy genes (sparing the babies from sometimes-fatal diseases), 100% of the treated pregnancies. Up to 2 year followup, normal development so far.

Summary: First conversion of a human somatic cell into an egg cell which then produced an embryo, though with low success rates and all embryos aneuploid.
Commentary: First conversion of a human somatic cell into an egg cell which then produced an embryo. The main problem before was that doing nuclear transfer (as many have before) leaves you with too many chromosomes. A few novelties involved: first “mitomeiosis” (electroporation + roscovitine). Their new method of artificial fertilisation gets close to natural baseline (78% egg activation rate). But 9% embryogenesis success rate and all embryos were aneuploid. Random chromosome segregation, no crossover recombination. Long way off from usable in vitro gametogenesis.

Summary: Two distinct non-natural antibodies derived from pre-existing natural antibodies serve as an extremely effective antivenom.
Trevogrumab is a drug that could potentially build muscle in lazy or elderly people.
Commentary: The unusually badly-named Trevogrumab invites us to speculate about a world in which everyone, no matter how lazy or elderly, is jacked. Wine pairing for Ozempic.

Summary: Just drain a haematoma through a needle rather than brain surgery.
Commentary: Just drain a haematoma through a needle rather than brain surgery

Summary: A new method reveals how transcription factors bind not just to single high-affinity DNA sites but to overlapping networks of weaker ones
A new scaleable method for synthesizing stable gold
Commentary: Your prior is going to be understandably low on this, but I know this team

Summary: Living things have glowing auras (a 10^−7 V field releasing weak photons).
Commentary: living things have glowing auras (a 10^−7 V field releasing weak photons)

Summary: A protein language model generates new "superior" Yamanaka factors with a suggestion hit rate >30%, compared to <10% in normal screening.
Commentary: A protein language model generates new “superior” Yamanaka factors. Suggestion hit rate was >30%, compared to <10% in normal screening.

Summary: BDNF, a growth factor in the brain, may be a fast-acting depression treatment
In areas with toxic levels of natural fluoride (mostly China), observational studies find that children have lower IQ scores.
Commentary: A meta-analysis of the fluoride-IQ link using mostly just China and India studies involving massive overdoses. For water fluoride <1.5 mg/L (WHO guideline), the association is null. Harm only appears when using urinary fluoride measures. But urinary fluoride conflates total exposure from all sources (water, food, toothpaste, tea, pollution from coal burning). You can't isolate the effect of water fluoridation. I² = 94%) suggests they're pooling fundamentally different studies.

Summary: A debunking of the link between fluoride and lowered IQ
First recorded instance of "xenoparity", an animal giving birth to offspring of a different species.
Commentary: First recorded instance of “xenoparity”, an animal giving birth to offspring of a different species. Began as parasitism and evolved into cross-species cloning for clean genome-level separation of fertility

Summary: A new flu drug might outperform vaccines.
Commentary: A new flu drug might outperform vaccines:

Summary: An E. coli vaccine is currently undergoing Phase III human testing.
Commentary: E. coli is the second-most lethal bacterium in the world, with about a million deaths a year. There are currently no effective vaccines for it. An E. coli vaccine is currently undergoing Phase III human testing. About 10% of food poisoning cases are in the crosshairs

Summary: Challenge trial on a salmonella vaccine showed roughly 70% effectiveness.
Commentary: Challenge trial (n=72) on a salmonella vaccine showed roughly 70% effectiveness. There are currently no effective vaccines for it. 2 million fever cases a year, 150k deaths.

Summary: Senescence-resistant human mesenchymal progenitor cells counter aging in primates
Commentary: Aging

Summary: Tiny test (n=16) of a pancreatic cancer vaccine has good results
Commentary: Tiny test (n=16) of a pancreatic cancer vaccine has good results, recurrence dropping from 80% to 30%. 3 year followup.

Summary: Nankai University team claim to have transplanted cloned islet cells (the insulin-producing parts of the pancreas) into a patient and she's ok after a year.
Commentary: Nankai University team claim to have transplanted cloned islet cells (the insulin-producing parts of the pancreas) into a patient and she's ok after a year

Summary: First successful transplant of a non-human lung into a human.
Commentary: Given a shortage of donors, this could allow many more patients to receive transplants.

Summary: Claim that we have identified a bacterium that causes schizophrenia.
Commentary: Claim that we have identified a bacterium that causes schizophrenia. Schizophrenia is heritable, but we have struggled to identify the genes involved, only explaining 32% of the variance with monstrously large polygenic scoring. Skolnick instead proposes that one unusual gut bacterium, Ruminococcus gnavus, is responsible through a specific amine pathway, effectively drugging you constantly over your life. MAYBE MAYBE this means it's curable. Obvious test is to see what happens (has already happened) in patients who have schizophrenia and also get full-spectrum antibiotics for other reasons.

Summary: Some new drugs treating long covid as an acquired autoimmune disorder
Commentary: Some new drugs treating long covid as an acquired autoimmune disorder, which makes sense to me. JAK inhibitors. 20%?

Summary: There is going to be the first clinical trial for a cure to a prion disease
Generative biology on the way. They generated 285 new phage virus genomes with a DNA language model.
Commentary:
They generated 285 new phage virus genomes with a DNA language model. 16 of them successfully attacked the target E. coli strain and (crucially) not other strains. “The viable generated phages harbored hundreds of novel mutations, many of which do not map to any sequence seen in nature.”
The old interpolation problem crops up here too though: the AI-generated phage genomes were all >90% similar to existing phages.
30%

Summary: AI for antibiotic design
Commentary: AI for antibiotic design

Summary: “Bridge editing” is a possible replacement for CRISPR with less mess and damage.
Commentary: “Bridge editing” is a possible replacement for CRISPR with less mess and damage. “write massive changes into the right place in the human genome”. There’s a naturally occurring recombinase enzyme that is more programmable than CRISPR because it doesn’t rely on noisy cell repair mechanisms coming in to actually do the intended edit. This paper found one that works ok in human cells and then engineered it intensely and then tried excising a disease gene. Insertions, excisions, and inversions at scale. It’s still only 82% specificity, but CRISPR was at 30% at a similar point in its development.40%

Summary: Generating antibodies against specific protein targets.
Commentary: Generating antibodies against specific protein targets. If you test 70 designs per antigen you succeed around 10% of the time (pass@70). Notably this was all inference compute (300 H100-hours per run) on AbMPNN and AlphaFold3, no new model training. (AbMPNN is already finetuned for antibody prediction.)40%

Summary: Biggest ever run of de novo antibody design and in silico screening.
Commentary: Actually useful AI: “binder design”. biggest ever run of de novo antibody design and in silico screening. A specialised version of AlphaFold2, mBER. 1.15 million candidate antibodies. 145 targets, 45% of which got a “design success” binder, and in vitro a 38% binding rate. Open sourced.

Summary: Lab grown salmon has been approved in the US.
A vegan cheese beat dairy in a big competition
Evo 2 genome modelling
7,000-year-old mummy DNA has revealed a ‘ghost’ branch of humanity
Genetically engineering mosquitoes to not transmit malaria.
Commentary: Genetically engineering mosquitos to not transmit malaria

Summary: 5-MeO-DMT trial for treatment-resistant depression showed large effect sizes.
Commentary: 5-MeO-DMT trial for treatment-resistant depression is in. Supposedly d=1. Toad venom for what ails you. Recall that major depression is maybe 2% of the total global burden of disease.

Summary: SGLT2 inhibitors could be a new medical Swiss Army knife
New 90% effective malaria vaccine.
Skin cells turned into egg cells, possibly enabling gay couples to have bio children.
Press release gives a new gene therapy AMT-130 a putative 75% slowdown in Huntingdon's progression over 3 years.
Commentary:
Press release gives a new gene therapy AMT-130 a putative 75% slowdown in Huntingdon’s progression over 3 years. Also the press release seems to contradict itself. > In the U.S. study, a total of 26 patients with early manifest Huntington’s disease were randomized to treatment (n=6 low dose; n=10 high dose) or an imitation (sham) procedure (n=10)
No proper results up yet

Summary: The case study underlying Festinger's classic cognitive dissonance backfire theory does not support the theory
Retraction of a 2022 Science paper claiming "direct imaging of neuronal activity,” (DIANA), using fMRI to capture individual neuronal activity
De facto retraction of a 2024 economics paper claiming many things about AI speedups in materials science
40% of animal studies of brain bleeding had problematic/duplicated images
Ozempic could reverse ageing
Circulatronics: towards a microscopic brain implant, a hybrid of immune cells and electronics, that you can inject and which self-implants in a particular region
Commentary: In mice

Summary: A programmable substrate
Commentary: "If successful, psychiatry would gain for mental states what CRISPR gave genetics, what mRNA gave vaccines, and what CAR-T gave immunology: A programmable substrate”

Summary: Supposed marker of life outside the solar system
Commentary: This seems desperate to me

Summary: Signs of low-temperature redox reactions in a Mars rock
Commentary: More wishful Life on Mars talk. Signs of low-temperature redox reactions in a Mars rock

Summary: Bonobos have compositional language.
First human infant to successfully receive a personalized gene therapy for his rare incurable SNP disease
Commentary: carbamoyl-phosphate synthetase 1 deficiency, a disease with an estimated 50% mortality in early infancy,

Summary: Orb is an AI simulator for chemical potentials allowing for thousands of simultaneous molecules, running well enough on a Macbook.
Commentary: Missed last year: Orb is an AI simulator for chemical potentials allowing for thousands of simultaneous molecules. Runs well enough on a Macbook.

Summary: A nematode survived being frozen in permafrost for 46,000 years and resumed reproduction once thawed, potentially enabling applications for organ and food preservation.
Commentary: A nematode survived being frozen in permafrost for 46,000 years - and resumed reproduction once thawed. If we are able to figure out how it does this, it could have applications for organ preservation and food preservation.

Summary: China built and spun up the world's first-ever thorium molten salt reactor
Proton energies achievable in laser accelerators could be tripled by using specially designed micronozzle targets
The first evidence of a solar take-off in Africa
Tritium breeder breakthrough
Nuclear waste could be a source of fuel in future reactors
First synthesis of hexanitrogen, the most energy-dense molecule ever synthesised, which could be an incredibly useful rocket fuel
Commentary: First synthesis of hexanitrogen, the most energy-dense molecule ever synthesised. The synthesis works at room temperature, but it breaks down after 36 milliseconds, but that’s long enough to cool it normally and so keep it stable indefinitely. “it would be the most useful rocket fuel on the planet. N6 would not burn with a flame: it’s just a burst of energy that generates a large volume [of gas] – so a lot of thrust – and it is non-corrosive”.

Summary: A high-field quasi-isodynamic stellarator for a prototypical fusion power plant
Commentary: Fusion

Summary: A proposal to employ a buried nuclear explosion in a remote basaltic seabed for pulverizing basalt, thereby accelerating carbon sequestration through Enhanced Rock Weathering (ERW).
Infrared contact lenses allow people to see in the dark, including with their eyes closed.
Commentary: Infrared contact lenses allow people to see in the dark, including with their eyes closed.

Summary: New twist on PET scans unlocks hidden signals for diagnosing disease
New type of microscope using a normal continuous wave laser instead of expensive ultrafast femtosecond-pulse laser systems, making it 40x cheaper.
Commentary: New type of microscope using a normal continuous wave laser instead of expensive ultrafast femtosecond-pulse laser systems. 40x cheaper machine, maybe 100x cheaper beam. Tested for now with one particular fluorescent protein. “optical sectioning” - seeing inside tissue noninvasively. Preprint “Shooting lasers at mice brains”.

Summary: Electric vehicles now last just as long as petrol ones.
Commentary: “2 photon microscope, using dramatically cheaper light sources. In the limit this tech could image single neurons at the scale of the whole cortex and every synapse of an individual neuron.”

Summary: Scalable solar desalination: a grid of porous aerogels.
Commentary: Scalable solar desalination: a grid of porous aerogels

Summary: Faster semiconductor
The first high-bandwidth neuromotor interface with performant out-of-the-box generalization across people.
A new quantum algorithm computes topological knot invariants and could one day be used for DNA, proteins, polymers, chemistry, and quantum computing.
Commentary: New quantum algorithm, for computing topological knot invariants. Not general, since it requires spectral gap assumptions. Complexity uncertain; they give preliminary numerical evidence for it being polynomial-time. Could one day be used for DNA, proteins, polymer, cool chemistry, and (ha) quantum computing. There are now 75 known quantum algorithms.

Summary: Sets containing line segments in all directions must have full dimension in 3D, with connection

summary: Planning in imagination" (offline simulation) allows robot to learn to walk in 1 hour with no resets
commentary: planning in imagination" (offline simulation) allows robot to learn to walk in 1 hour with no resets

summary: AI-designed face paint inspired by Juggalos could potentially fool the 15,000 facial recognition cameras at the Qatar World Cup

commentary: Sadly one must be cautious when reading Deepmind PR

summary: The efficacy of the R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccine reached 75%+ efficacy.
commentary: I was disappointed in last year's malaria vaccine (RTS,S) but R21 might be the real deal: best guess is ~80% protection (nonrobust, sample had very few deaths). way more scalable too. One of humanity's oldest, most pitiless enemies, on his way out

summary: Deepmind: Accelerating race against antibiotic resistance
commentary: Sadly one must be cautious when reading Deepmind PR

summary: Deepmind: Targeting early-onset Parkinson’s with AI
commentary: Sadly one must be cautious when reading Deepmind PR

summary: Rigorous simulation of a new kind of computer not limited by the usual physical constraints and close to reversible
commentary: Good news for the year 2323: rigorous sim of a new kind of computer not limited by the usual physical constraints and close to reversible (i.e. free operations).Biggest story of the year on the long view

summary: Conclusive evidence that Mars is geologically active (specificaly, has a mantle plume)
commentary: Conclusive evidence that Mars is geologically active (specificaly, has a mantle plume)

summary: AI taking a folded protein structure and predicting what amino acid led to it
commentary: Actually useful AI: what it's doing in biomed

summary: 5,562 proteins were found to have tissue-specific enrichment across 32 human tissues.
commentary: There are differences in the corresponding RNA vs protein abundance levels across tissues (potentially pointing to protein secretion). This discordance is especially prominent in the brain (e.g., for vesicular transport proteins that are involved in neurotransmitter function and cell-cell signaling). Tissue enrichment patterns of proteins are related to genetic disease phenotypes which can't be inferred from the transcriptome alone.

summary: A passive solar desalination device with record efficiency.
commentary: a passive solar desalination device with record efficiency. Supposedly 4 litres of potable water an hour, few precision components
